description: Travel planning — first trip to Japan

turns.content: I'm planning a 10-day trip to Japan in March. First time visiting. Where should I go?

March is excellent timing — you might catch early cherry blossoms. For a first trip, the Tokyo-Kyoto-Osaka circuit is classic for a reason: urban Japan, traditional Japan, and food culture respectively. A rough 10-day split: 4 nights Tokyo, 1 night Hakone (Mt Fuji views), 3 nights Kyoto, 2 nights Osaka. The shinkansen connecting them is part of the experience, and a 7-day JR Pass covers inter-city travel economically.
Is March crowded? I'm a bit worried about tourist crowds.

Honestly yes — March is peak season, especially if sakura blooms early. Fushimi Inari and Arashiyama in Kyoto can feel overwhelming midday. The workaround: go early. At 7am even the most famous spots are quiet and genuinely beautiful. It is also worth building in a day in less-visited Nara — 45 minutes from Kyoto, free-roaming deer, stunning temples, dramatically less crowded. One night in a traditional ryokan in a smaller town like Kinosaki Onsen provides a wonderful contrast with Tokyo.
What about food — I eat vegetarian. Is that difficult in Japan?

It requires some planning but is very manageable. The main challenge is hidden dashi stock in broths. Kyoto is actually the best city for this — shojin ryori, traditional Buddhist cuisine, is entirely plant-based and deeply rooted in Kyoto culture. Tenryuji temple in Arashiyama offers a stunning shojin meal. For everyday eating, convenience store onigiri have clear ingredients and most ramen shops in Tokyo now have a vegan broth option. Download the HappyCow app before you go.
